ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for spicy breaded chicken tenders; closest parent 'fried chicken food product' (FOODON_00002490) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (chicken meat, coated and fried) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for spicy chicken tenders exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for spicy breaded chicken tenders; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of 3 spicy chicken tenders weighs approximately 135 g, equivalent to three individual breaded and fried chicken strips as served at Jollibee Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken tenders, breaded, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21069 / FDC ID 172960)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for breaded fried chicken strips used as the closest approved analogue. Values represent a generic breaded fried chicken tender profile. Spicy seasoning adds negligible macronutrient change but may slightly elevate sodium; sodium value adjusted upward by approximately 10% to reflect typical fast-food spicy seasoning based on USDA Branded Food comparators. Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate values are consistent with the USDA SR Legacy reference for this food category.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021;114(5):1625–1632.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for spicy chicken tenders. GI of 55 is estimated from the analogous food category 'breaded fried chicken' as referenced in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 Atkinson et al. international GI tables. The low GI reflects the wheat-flour breading component, with protein and fat content of the chicken significantly blunting postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 20.0) / 100 ≈ 11.0, where carbohydrate per 135 g serving ≈ 20.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~702 mg per 3-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of spicy chicken tenders due to the high sodium load from seasoning and breading, which can contribute meaningfully to the recommended daily sodium ceiling of 2000 mg; one serving provides approximately 35% of that limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (55) and moderate glycemic load (~11 per serving) from wheat-flour breading, coupled with high fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume spicy chicken tenders in moderation; the wheat-flour breading contributes a low glycemic load of approximately 11 per serving, and the high fat content may impair insulin sensitivity if consumed frequently as part of a high-calorie fast-food pattern.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), high sodium, and deep-frying in vegetable oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of deep-fried breaded chicken tenders due to their saturated fat, residual trans fat, and high sodium content, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and elevated blood pressure when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Chicken Tenders (3 pieces) is a fast-food menu item consisting of three individual strips of chicken breast meat that have been marinated in a spicy seasoning blend — typically including chilli, paprika, garlic powder, and black pepper — coated in a seasoned wheat-flour breading, and deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy. The product is sold at Jollibee Singapore (Bugis Village branch) and is characteristic of the Filipino-American fast-food chain's fried chicken range. The exterior is crunchy and well-seasoned with noticeable heat, while the interior remains juicy and tender. Each 3-piece serving weighs approximately 135 g and delivers around 332 kcal, 23.6 g protein, 17.8 g fat, and 20.0 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is elevated at approximately 702 mg per serving, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), driven primarily by the wheat-flour breading, though the high protein and fat content moderates the postprandial glucose response. From a clinical nutrition perspective, this item is energy-dense and sodium-rich, and should be consumed in moderation by individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It is certified Halal and contains gluten from the wheat breading. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for Jollibee Singapore's Spicy Chicken Tenders was available from an approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Food entry for this specific product). Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried chicken strips (FDC ID 172960), which is the closest approved analogue. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for breaded fried chicken. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172960 (chicken tenders, breaded, fried) as no exact Jollibee-specific nutritional panel was available from an approved source. Sodium has been adjusted upward by approximately 10% to reflect typical fast-food spicy seasoning levels, consistent with USDA Branded Food comparators for similar products. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy reference entry for this food category. 3. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy reference entry for this food category. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy reference entry for this food category. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy reference entry for this food category. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — deep-fried breaded chicken contains no meaningful vitamin C. 7. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on the breaded fried chicken category in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. The low GI reflects the wheat-flour breading component. 8. This food is tagged Halal per field survey cuisine tags and Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification. 9. Serving size of 135 g is estimated as 3 × 45 g per tender, consistent with typical fast-food chicken tender weights. 10. This is a Fast Food cuisine item served in Singapore; multilingual names are provided in English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Veritas multilingual rules for Singapore-served dishes.

Iteration 1: Documented vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy.

Iteration 1: Verified aliases_by_language entries are all present verbatim in flat aliases list.
Iteration 1: Used only match_type values 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none' in ontology section.

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per standard GI classification rules (GI <= 55 is low). Updated relevant notes and descriptions to reflect this change.
